Quality System Specialist - Field Action (FA) Process
description:
**Additional Locations:** Italy\-Milan; Spain\-Madrid; United Kingdom\-Hemel Hempstead

**Diversity \- Innovation \- Caring \- Global Collaboration \- Winning Spirit \- High Performance**


At Boston Scientific, we’ll give you the opportunity to harness all that’s within you by working in teams of diverse and high\-performing employees, tackling some of the most important health industry challenges. With access to the latest tools, information and training, we’ll help you in advancing your skills and career. Here, you’ll be supported in progressing – whatever your ambitions.

 **Background**


We are seeking an additional resource to support Field Action execution and to drive ongoing FA related process improvement initiatives, within the European, Middle East and Africa (EMEA) Region.

 **Role Overview**


The Quality System Specialist will play a pivotal role as a Regional Field Action Coordinator (RFAC), supporting the EMEA Field Action Process. This individual will be part of a dedicated team responsible for planning, executing, and closing field actions, as well as identifying and implementing process improvement initiatives in collaboration with impacted stakeholders.

  

This role involves close partnership with Global, Regional, and in\-country Quality FA teams, as well as cross\-functional stakeholders in Marketing, Regulatory Affairs, and Customer Care. Success in this position requires excellent interpersonal skills and stakeholder management experience, with a strong focus on process efficiency, while ensuring adherence to quality system requirements that meet Boston Scientific, customer, and regulatory expectations.

  

This a a hybrid position, which means working three days from the office and two days from home.

 **Key Responsibilities**

* Manage the EMEA Regional Field Action process execution steps in accordance with defined procedures.
* Collaborate with cross\-functional teams (Regulatory, Quality, Customer Care) to ensure stakeholder understanding and expectations in relation to Field Action activities.
* Represent EMEA Quality Regional FA Team in cross\-functional meetings and improvement workstreams
* Support the execution of EMEA FA process improvement initiatives.
* Communicate effectively across all levels of the organisation, tailoring messaging to audience.
* Influence decision\-making through clear articulation of risks, trade\-offs, and business impact.
* Assist with audits and inspections related to field action processes.
* Ensure timely and accurate documentation of NCEP/CAPA investigations in accordance with established procedures in relation to the Field Action Process.

 **Qualifications \& Requirements**

* 5–7 years of experience in Quality System Management within the medical device or related industry.
* Knowledge of the Field Action execution process.
* Demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively in a global, matrixed organization.
* Experience leading cross\-functional initiatives to completion.
* Ability to work independently, and within a team, in a dynamic environment.
* Strong interpersonal, communication, and influencing skills.
* Excellent decision\-making, prioritization, problem\-solving, and time management abilities.
* High sense of urgency and commitment to execution in a fast\-paced setting.

 **Requisition ID:** 629247

  

As a leader in medical science for more than 40 years, we are committed to solving the challenges that matter most – united by a deep caring for human life. Our mission to advance science for life is about transforming lives through innovative medical solutions that improve patient lives, create value for our customers, and support our employees and the communities in which we operate. Now more than ever, we have a responsibility to apply those values to everything we do – as a global business and as a global corporate citizen.

  

So, choosing a career with Boston Scientific (NYSE: BSX) isn’t just business, it’s personal. And if you’re a natural problem\-solver with the imagination, determination, and spirit to make a meaningful difference to people worldwide, we encourage you to apply and look forward to connecting with you!
company_description: Boston Scientific is dedicated to transforming lives through innovative medical solutions that improve the health of patients around the world.

title: Health Economics and Outcome Research (HEOR) Manager
description:
***Lead change that lasts.***


At Mölnlycke, we believe that real impact starts with people. People who are bold enough to challenge the status quo. People who care deeply for their work, their colleagues, and the world around them. People who know exactly why they do what they do.

  

As a global medical solutions company, we design and deliver innovative products and services across wound care, operating room solutions, gloves, and antiseptics. Our mission is to advance performance in healthcare by supporting professionals, improving outcomes, and making a difference where it matters most.

  

In Wound Care at Mölnlycke, we help to prevent, manage and accelerate the healing of wounds.

 **Where You'll Make a Difference**


Mölnlycke is now looking for a ***Health Economics \& Outcomes Research (HEOR) Manager*** to join our Clinical, Medical \& Professional Affairs (CMPA) leadership team.

  

We have world\-class clinical evidence. Now we need someone to bridge those two worlds: to ensure that every study we design, every evidence programme we build, speaks not just to clinical outcomes but to economic value, the kind of value that moves payers, health systems, and hospital budget holders.

  

This role sits on the CMPA Leadership Team, giving you the reach and influence to shape evidence strategy, study execution, and stakeholder education across the entire department. You will be the person who future\-proofs Mölnlycke's evidence portfolio for a world where economic proof is just as important as clinical proof.

 **Here's how you'll make an impact:**

* Define and lead Mölnlycke's global HEOR strategy, ensuring alignment with business objectives and market access requirements across all regions.
* Embed health economic endpoints and value\-based evidence into clinical study designs from the very start
* Lead the design and oversight of value\-based evidence programmes for products and integrated solutions.
* Partner cross\-functionally with Clinical, Regulatory, Market Access, and Commercial teams to ensure HEOR insights drive real decisions.
* Build HEOR capability across CMPA and Clinical Engagement teams, acting as mentor, educator, and subject matter authority for colleagues developing their health economics knowledge.
* Ensure health economic evidence is woven into Mölnlycke's professional education programmes for external stakeholders.
* Select appropriate analytical frameworks, economic models, and data sources for health economic evaluations.

 **What You'll Get**

**Your growth and development**


This is a rare opportunity to establish a function, mentor colleagues, and shape evidence strategy at a global scale. As the HEOR capability grows, so does the potential of this role.

**Flexibility, wellbeing and benefits**

* Permanent position (European base strongly preferred).
* Fully remote\-compatible role within a virtual global team, strong online presence and collaboration skills are key.
* Competitive compensation package aligned to local market and seniority level.

 **What You Bring**

**Must\-haves**

* Advanced degree (Master's or PhD) in Health Economics, Outcomes Research, or a closely related discipline.
* Deep, hands\-on expertise in health economic evaluation methods, cost\-effectiveness analysis, budget impact modelling, real\-world evidence generation.
* Minimum 5 years of experience in health economics and outcomes research within the medical device or healthcare industry.
* Medical device (MedTech) background highly preferred.
* Knowledge of global HTA requirements and payer evidence needs (NICE, HAS, G\-BA and equivalents).
* Exceptional communication and influencing skills, you will be building a new way of thinking across a global, matrixed organisation. Fluency in English (written and spoken) is essential.
* Comfortable operating as a leader without direct authority, influencing through expertise, credibility and relationship\-building.

**Nice\-to\-haves**

* Experience in wound care or wound management is an asset, but not required.
* Familiarity with IEC 62366 and US FDA evidence requirements is a plus.
* Experience building a HEOR function or capability from the ground up would be a strong differentiator.

 **Our Approach to Diversity \& Inclusion**


We're building a workplace where everyone feels seen, heard, and valued. We believe that a diverse mix of people — from different cultures, ages, geographies, and genders — makes us stronger. It brings fresh perspectives, drives better decisions, and helps us deliver solutions that reflect the world we serve. Inclusion isn't just a value — it's how we work, grow, and lead together.

 **About Mölnlycke**


Mölnlycke is a world\-leading medical products and solutions company that equips healthcare professionals to achieve the best patient, clinical and economic outcomes.

  

Our business is organised in the four business areas Wound Care, Operating Room Solutions, Gloves and Antiseptics, where customer centricity, sustainability and digitalisation are at the heart of everything we do.

  

Mölnlycke employs around 8,700 people. The company headquarters are in Gothenburg, Sweden and we operate in more than 100 countries worldwide. Mölnlycke is a founding partner of GoCo Health Innovation City, a world\-class innovation cluster for health.

  

Since 2007, the company has been part of Investor AB, an engaged owner of high\-quality, global companies which was founded by the Wallenberg family in 1916\. For more information, please visit www.molnlycke.com and www.molnlycke.com/careers.

title: National Patient Access Manager
description:
**Company Description** ***Statistics show that women and underrepresented groups tend to apply to jobs only if they meet 100% of the qualifications. Sobi encourages you to change that statistic and apply. Rarely do candidates meet 100% of the qualifications. We look forward to your application!***


At Sobi, each person brings their unique talents to work as a team and make a difference. We are dedicated to developing and delivering innovative therapies to improve the lives of people who live with a rare disease. Our edge comes from our team of people and our commitment to patients.

 **Job Description**  

Contribute to the definition and implementation of access strategies for Sobi’s portfolio in Italy, supporting the goal of ensuring that the value of therapies for rare diseases is recognized and that patients can access them in a timely and sustainable way. The role requires a combination of strategic thinking, cross\-functional collaboration, and hands\-on expertise in P\&R dossiers, HEOR models, and payer value materials — fully aligned with Sobi’s values: Care, Ambition, Partnership, and Ownership.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Support the definition and execution of Market Access and Pricing \& Reimbursement strategies for Sobi products, in alignment with local and global priorities.
* Prepare and coordinate Pricing \& Reimbursement (P\&R) dossiers, ensuring the quality and robustness of clinical, economic, and value arguments.
* Review and adapt HEOR models (cost\-effectiveness, budget impact) to meet the requirements of health authorities and payers.
* Develop and communicate effective payer value propositions, translating clinical and economic evidence into clear and relevant messages for decision\-makers.
* Contribute to the creation of institutional communication messages \& materials (position papers, presentations) to support interactions with institutions, payers, and external stakeholders.
* Collaborate closely with cross\-functional teams (Patient Access, Medical Affairs, Regulatory, Commercial, CE\&C) to ensure consistency and integration of access strategies.
* Monitor and interpret the evolution of the regulatory, HTA, and healthcare policy environment, identifying trends and opportunities to anticipate access barriers.
* Contribute to internal knowledge sharing on access, pricing, and policy processes, fostering alignment and team development.

  

**Qualifications**  

* Academic background: Bachelor’s Scientific Degree, Health policy or Economics are preferred
* Minimum of 5 years of pharmaceutical industry and \+3 years in market access positions


Technical skills:

* Experience in rare diseases or specialty care is considered a strong plus.
* In\-depth knowledge of Pricing \& Reimbursement processes at national and regional levels, with direct experience in dossier submission.
* Familiarity with HEOR modelling, HTA, and policy shaping.


Soft skills:

* Excellent analytical, synthesis, and communication skills, both written and verbal.
* Fluent in English and Italian.

  

**Additional Information**  

All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

title: Junior Medical Affairs Manager
description:
**Sinclair is Hiring! Join Our Team as Junior Medical Affairs Manager**


The Junior Medical Affairs Manager (EU) supports the Medical Affairs function in delivering scientific excellence, evidence generation, and compliant medical communication for our injectable aesthetic portfolio. The role focuses on supporting HCP education, medical information, and local/regional medical activities across Europe, ensuring alignment with global medical strategy and local regulatory requirements. The role contributes to the development of scientific content and communication formats for relevant target groups, supports competitive intelligence activities, and provides input on the continuous improvement of internal Medical Affairs processes and KPIs.

**Location is flexible across Europe**: Spain, UK, Italy, Netherlands, Germany, Portugal, Poland, France, Bulgaria.

 **About Sinclair**


Founded in 1971, Sinclair is a global medical aesthetics organisation, that delivers an extensive product range. With an in\-house commercial infrastructure, including manufacturing and a network of distributors in leading global markets, our products are sold in 55 countries worldwide.


This is a great time to join Sinclair as we continue to increase our product range and expand into new markets and territories.

##### **Sinclair Values:**


**Act with Integrity** Consistently doing the right thing even when it’s the hard choice; 100% Compliance with all rules, standard operating procedures and guidelines  

**Results\-Driven** Make a business impact in all you do, whether sales, efficiency, operational excellence; it should make a meaningful impact  

**Innovation\-Centered** Redefining Aesthetics, we must be pioneering in how we do business; this can be in products, in service models, or strategy


**Own It!** Be Accountable for your decisions, actions and consequences; Be Reliable to your customers and colleagues

#### **What you will be doing**

**Medical Information and Scientific Support**

* Provide accurate, timely, and compliant medical information to internal stakeholders, field teams, and external inquiries from healthcare professionals.
* Serve as a scientific resource for product data, literature, and device\-related information related to injectables used in aesthetic procedures.

**Medical Education and KOL Engagement**

* Support development and execution of medical education programs, speaker training, case\-based discussions, and non\-promotional educational activities.
* Support to identify, engage, and maintain relationships with key opinion leaders (KOLs) and regional experts in aesthetic medicine, dermatology, and related fields; assist with KOL mapping and management plans.
* Support the evaluation, planning, and execution of selected medical congresses and scientific symposia, and contribute to the organisation of Advisory Boards under supervision.

**Evidence Generation and Lifecycle Management**

* Support local observational studies, registries, post\-marketing commitments, and real\-world evidence initiatives under supervision.
* Assist in the preparation of scientific communications, posters, abstracts, white papers, and regulatory\-required documentation.

**Regulatory and Compliance Support**

* Ensure Medical Affairs activities comply with regional regulations, industry codes (e.g., EFPIA/ABPI equivalents in region), and company policies.
* Assist with the review of promotional and non\-promotional materials for scientific accuracy and compliance; support pharmacovigilance activities as needed.

**Cross\-Functional Collaboration**

* Partner with Clinical Development, Regulatory, Marketing, Sales, Pharmacovigilance, and Medical Information to implement regional medical strategies.
* Support training for sales teams on product science, safety profiles, and clinical data.

**Literature Surveillance and Competitive Intelligence**

* Monitor relevant scientific literature, guidelines, and competitor activity to inform medical strategy and respond to inquiries.
* Contribute to safety signal monitoring activities, provide input on safety updates, and escalate concerns per local procedure.
* Support regular analyses of competitor product communication and prepare findings in a structured format for relevant internal stakeholders.

**Documentation and Record Keeping**

* Maintain accurate records of medical activities, meetings, and approved materials in regional systems according to policy.
* Contribute to the continuous improvement of internal Medical Affairs processes and cross\-functional collaboration, and support the definition and tracking of relevant KPIs to evaluate and refine medical activities.

##### **Your skills and experience**

**Essential:**

* Medical Degree or Master degree in a scientific discipline or higher
* Experience: 1\-3 years in Medical Affairs, Medical Information, or related field within the pharmaceutical/medical device industry.
* Keen interest in developing and maintaining expert knowledge for assigned therapeutic area/product and in medical research in general.
* Knowledge of the scientific methods applied to clinical research and the current legislative/regulatory controls.
* Strong commitment to compliance with the relevant rules and procedures, and to scientific quality and integrity.
* Ability to handle complex medical and scientific information confidently, and to prepare content in a clear, structured and easily understandable way.
* Analytical mindset, strong problem\-solving skills and a proactive, self\-directed and reliable way of working.
* Comfortable using digital communication channels and modern collaboration tools, with the ability to work confidently across different formats and systems.
* Team player with empathy, adaptability and drive, contributing to a collaborative working environment and shared results.
* Excellent English

**Desirable:**

* First exposure to the legal framework for medical devices or pharmaceutical products (e.g. MDR 2017/745, German HWG /French Loi Bertrand or equivalent national regulations).
* Solid knowledge of the pharmaceutical environment and excellent skills to set stake\-holders relationship.
* Medical aesthetics/devices experience
* Any other European language (German, French, Spanish, Italian)
* Experience with injectables and/or dermatology/aesthetic medicine is highly desirable

**We have a real focus on developing our people, and by fostering an entrepreneurial culture, we encourage flexibility, accountability and autonomy. The company is full of opportunity for those who wish to grasp it. Our people thrive on engagement, development and a varied workload. You will often be included in projects that require you to collaborate cross\-functionally.**


This is an exciting opportunity to join a successful company, with big ambitions for the future.


If this sounds like you and you meet the requirements, please apply, we would love to hear from you.


Please note if we receive a high volume of applications, we may close the role before the closing date noted.


By making this application you give consent for personal information to be used in automated decision\-making processes relating to key job requirements which are stated in this ad.

 **Sinclair does not accept speculative or unsolicited CVs from Recruitment Agencies. Any unsolicited CVs received will be treated as property of Sinclair and Terms \& Conditions associated with the use of such CVs will be considered null and void**

===========================


As a Clinical Education Specialist Apprentice, you will play a key role in supporting hospital customers by delivering high\-quality product demonstrations, clinical training, and device instruction directly in the operating room. Your contribution will directly support clinical teams, enhance customer satisfaction, and ultimately benefit patient outcomes.  

Location : Central and Southern Italy**Job Description**
===================

**Key responsibilities under supervision include:**

**Clinical Training \& Education**

* Plan, coordinate, and deliver **customer trainings**, product demonstrations, and **initial and follow\-up device instructions** at hospital sites, primarily in the operating room.
* Adapt training content and delivery to **specific customer needs**, going beyond standard training requests.

* Serve as a trusted clinical and technical advisor, answering **in\-depth clinical and technical questions** related to the products.
* Support customer\-specific system configurations in close collaboration with the **sales and service teams**.

**Sales \& Business Support**

**Events \& Knowledge Sharing**

* Support **trade fairs, congresses, and professional conferences** with product demonstrations, training sessions, and promotional activities.
* Facilitate **knowledge transfer on radiation protection in the operating room**, ensuring safe and compliant clinical practices.

**Required Qualifications**

* Qualification as **Radiology Technician (TSRM)** *or* a **Degree in Engineering**
* Strong interest in **medical technology and clinical applications**
* Fluency in **Italian** and **English**
* Valid **driver’s license** and willingness to travel regularly within Italy
* Excellent **verbal and written communication skills**

Supervise and manage an assigned team of CPMs and their subsidiaries, who manage or support project management activities to mitigate risk, control cost, project schedule and ensure quality, time and budget deliverables are completed to the Sponsor's satisfaction in accordance with applicable SOPs, policies and practices. If you want to make a difference at the center of health, come join our innovative company and help us improve the lives of people and animals everywhere. Apply today!

**Job Details**
===============

As a member of Pharmalex Italian Market Access Team, the Manager will be involved in the development of several projects related to the different areas of the Patient Access Team (PAT): Regional Access \& Value; HTA \& special projects or Patient engagement; reporting to the Senior Manager and/or Patient Access Lead

* Coordinating analysts/interns activities;
* Performing quantitative and qualitative analyses (landscape, GAP analysis, etc.) to aid development of recommendations;
* Perform the activities related to the preparation, of a regional dossier;
* Preparation of a value proposition or other materials to communicate the value of a drug;
* Taking responsibility for creating/reviewing reports, presentations and other client deliverables;
* Conducting desk research to a specific topic using internal and external sources;
* Support the company in business development;
* Monitoring AIFA website, AIFA decrees for drugs and regional decrees.

.

**Experience:**

**Nice to Have**

* Experience in Health Economics

**Skills \& Competencies**

title: Senior Researcher HEC
description:
In Ipsos Doxa siamo appassionati di persone, mercati, brand e società. La nostra ambizione è diventare partner di tutti i clienti che vogliono comprendere in profondità e dettaglio la loro audience, i mercati, i consumatori, e le marche in un mondo in rapida e continua evoluzione.


Siamo un solido brand presente in 90 paesi e più di 20\.000 professionisti, con un posizionamento unico di esperienze specifiche che allo stesso tempo si integrano in team dedicati.


Crediamo che i nostri clienti ci chiedano molto di più di semplici dati. Hanno bisogno di un partner che lavori con loro per produrre informazioni puntuali e pertinenti e trasformarle in concrete strategie di business.


Questo è il motivo per cui i nostri esperti mettono al servizio di aziende e istituzioni tutta la propria competenza e capacità di ascolto, non solo per fornire le rilevazioni più accurate ma anche per adattarle alle specifiche esigenze.


Ci poniamo un unico obiettivo: quello di offrire a tutti i nostri clienti una comprensione totale di mercati, società e persone. Per fare ciò, usiamo un mix unico di competenze professionali, tecnologia e know\-how tecnico applicando ad ogni cosa che facciamo i principi di sicurezza, semplicità, velocità e concretezza. In definitiva, crediamo che alla base del successo vi sia una semplice verità: **YOU ACT BETTER WHEN YOU ARE SURE!**

**La posizione**

Per la nostra sede di Milano stiamo cercando un/a **Senior Researcher** da inserire all’interno della Service Line Healthcare (HEC).


La nostra service line è specializzata in ricerche quali – quantitative in ambito farmaceutico, OTC, RX e veterinario. Lavorare in Ipsos Healthcare significa relazionarsi con tutto il mondo Ipsos attraverso una contaminazione positiva e stimolante con ricercatori delle altre service line.


A livello global Ipsos Healthcare è uno dei più importanti istituti al mondo e vanta:


* Un team di oltre 1100 esperti, con background accademici e/o con expertise in molteplici aree di business
* La presenza in oltre 50 paesi al mondo con un team quali\- quantitativo specificatamente dedicato al mondo healthcare
* Lavoriamo per oltre 500 aziende farmaceutiche: tutte le più grandi companies a cui si aggiungono aziende biotech, operanti nei medical devices e nella diagnostica, connected health, digital health e altri ambiti della sanità.

**Cosa farai?**

All’interno della Service Line Healthcare, avrai l’opportunità di lavorare con un team di esperti e di esprimere e accrescere le tue capacità professionali gestendo in autonomia la ricerca nelle sue diverse fasi e la relazione con il cliente.


In particolare, avrai le seguenti responsabilità:


* realizzare, anche con il supporto di risorse junior, la singola ricerca in modo efficace/efficiente attraverso la gestione autonoma di tutto il flusso di lavoro;
* gestire direttamente la relazione con il cliente per ottenerne la piena soddisfazione, attraverso l’ascolto attivo di specifici bisogni/opportunità;
* sviluppare expertise su un metodo/ambito, diventandone l’owner/referente all’interno della SL;
* guidare le risorse affidate e svilupparne le competenze attraverso l'affiancamento e la formazione on the job.

**Chi cerchiamo?**

* Esperienza pregressa di almeno 4 anni nel settore Healthcare
* Piena padronanza degli strumenti metodologici per la ricerca quantitativa
* Ottimo utilizzo del pacchetto Office, in particolare Word e Power Point
* Inglese fluente

**Competenze trasversali**

* Spirito di iniziativa / proattività
* Orientamento al risultato e al problem solving
* Apertura mentale / capacità di uscire dalla comfort zone
* Flessibilità
* Team work

**Cosa troverai**

* un percorso altamente formativo all’interno di un Gruppo internazionale;
* un onboarding guidato e strutturato da Line Manager e HRBP per facilitare il tuo ingresso e inserimento in azienda;
* un ambiente di lavoro stimolante e molto attento alle persone e alle politiche in termini di ESG: Ambiente, Sostenibilità, People \- D\&I;
* ampia flessibilità di orario e di lavoro da remoto per gestire al meglio il tuo tempo
* partecipazione ad attività interne di Benessere aziendale;
* formazione continua on the job, accesso alla piattaforma e\-learning (Ipsos Training Center);
* un Team People \& Culture HR a tua disposizione con cui potrai sempre confrontarti in caso di bisogno;
* l'opportunità di lavorare a stretto contatto con persone esperte del settore

**Taking Responsibility**

Ipsos riconosce la necessità di costruire una cultura inclusiva che valorizzi l'individualità e le diverse prospettive di ciascun lavoratore e lavoratrice. Da oltre 40 anni, la nostra missione è generare e analizzare dati sulla società, sui mercati, sui marchi e sui comportamenti per fornire ai nostri clienti gli approfondimenti che migliorano la loro comprensione del mondo. Questo non potrebbe essere realizzato senza le persone di Ipsos che compilano e analizzano questi dati: essi sono l'essenza di ciò che siamo e di ciò che facciamo. Ci impegniamo a offrire pari opportunità, a creare un ambiente che promuova l'inclusione. Ipsos incoraggia il proprio personale ad agire in modo rispettoso e responsabile, in linea con il codice e la nostra policy in materia di diversità e inclusione, diritti umani, uguaglianza e civiltà.


**Il processo di selezione**

Una volta ricevuta la tua candidatura, ci prenderemo il tempo necessario per valutarla attentamente. Nel caso in cui il tuo profilo sia in linea per questa o altre posizioni presso l'azienda, ci metteremo in contatto con te. Tuttavia, nel caso in cui non ci sia una corrispondenza al momento, non preoccuparti! ci assicureremo di tenere in considerazione il tuo cv per future opportunità in linea con il tuo profilo e nel totale rispetto della normativa privacy e GDPR. Tutte le candidature saranno prese in considerazione per la posizione senza riguardo all'età, alla razza, al colore della pelle, alla religione, al sesso, all'orientamento sessuale, all'identità di genere, all'origine nazionale, allo status di rifugiato o a qualsiasi altra categoria protetta e non saranno discriminati sulla base della disabilità.


**I nostri valori**

Siamo *"Proud to be Ipsos"* perché crediamo fortemente nella nostra cultura aziendale, incarnando i valori di *Integrità – Collaborazione – Curiosità – Spirito Imprenditoriale – Attenzione al Cliente* nei rapporti con i colleghi, con i clienti e con la società.


Se ti riconosci nella nostra realtà e nei nostri valori, se hai l’ambizione di entrare a far parte di un’azienda dinamica e internazionale e desideri crescere insieme a noi, candidati ora e inizia il tuo viaggio nel mondo Ipsos!


*I dati personali raccolti ai fini del processo di selezione saranno trattati nel rispetto del Regolamento UE n. 679/2016\.*

title: Digital Asset Manager
description:
CapsLock builds exceptional **IT marketing solutions** for large partners from North America. We don’t just advertise our clients’ products or services — we build long\-term marketing solutions within the context of enduring partnerships.


Our people’s diversity and their ideas inspire the **innovation** that runs through everything we do, from unique technology to industry\-leading services and customer experiences. We believe teams that openly support and challenge each other are destined to win.

 **Role Overview**


We are looking for a **Digital Asset Manager** who goes beyond basic filing. This person will be a highly organized creative who loves order and understands how to turn a massive collection of photos and videos into a revenue\-driving engine for the team. You will be responsible for the meticulous organization, tagging, and proactive discovery of high\-potential materials within a library of tens of thousands of assets.

**Your mission** will beensuring every visual asset is fully utilized and that production efforts are laser\-focused on the specific visual components currently driving performance in our marketing campaigns. You will bridge the gap between raw content and winning ads by identifying hidden gems and maintaining a sophisticated visual taxonomy.

 **Your Responsibilities**

* Master the **Canto DAM system** to create intuitive folder structures and precise tagging.
* Move beyond simple filing to identify patterns in what makes a "good" asset based on previous campaign success.
* Regularly curate **Trend Portals** that cross\-pollinate winning visual styles between different product verticals.
* Develop and maintain a **Visual Language** for the agency, ensuring assets are tagged by psychological triggers (e.g., Trust, Safety) rather than just basic descriptions.
* Self\-direct content audits to spot inventory gaps (e.g., "We are scaling product X but have no drone footage") and notify production before bottlenecks occur.
* Maintain **Style Guides** to ensure every editor is using the correct, most up\-to\-date brand materials and best practices.
* Scan competitor ads to find **Lookalike** assets within our own library to replicate winning layouts.

  

Requirements:
  

* **3\+ years** managing large\-scale libraries for agencies, production houses, or e\-commerce brands.
* An exceptional eye for detail and the ability to look at a photo and identify three different marketing angles it could fit into.
* Ability to manage projects and conduct audits without constant oversight.
* This role suits a creative professional with a strong organizational mindset — **not** a technical or performance marketing background.
* Mandatory location in **Europe**.
* Strong communication skills, ownership, and a proactive approach to quality.

**Nice to Have**

* Basic knowledge of Adobe Premiere or Photoshop to understand the editor's workflow.
* Active use of **AI tools** to optimize your own work and asset discovery.

**Infrastructure \& Ecosystem**

* **Organization: DAM** (Digital Asset Management) systems (**Canto**).
* **Workflow: ClickUp** for task tracking and standard enforcement.

**﻿**

**﻿Why do we deserve your attention?**


Not only do we offer fair and competitive pay, but we also provide perks and benefits that support your well\-being and growth. Here are some of them:

* **Remote Work \-** we are a truly remote\-first company. You choose where you feel the most productive and comfortable to make an impact.

* **Paid Time Off \-** work\-life balance matters to us, and we support it with paid time off for rest, personal time, or simply doing what makes you happy.

* **Ongoing Learning \-** we believe in curiosity and growth. That’s why we support continuous learning with workshops, online courses, conferences, and certifications.

* **Home Office \-** we help you set up your perfect workspace by covering equipment and furniture expenses, so you can do your best work from home.

* **Physical Well\-Being \-** from gym memberships and yoga classes to home workout equipment or even a bicycle, we give you the flexibility to stay active your way.

* **Unlimited Sick Days \-** your health matters. Take the time you need to rest and recover \- we’ll be here when you’re ready to return.

* **Medical Coverage \-** we support you and your family with reimbursement for medical insurance and health\-related expenses, including dental care and psychological support.

* **Co\-Working Space \-** if you sometimes crave a change of scenery or more social interaction, we’ll cover the full cost of a co\-working space.

* **Fun Stuff \-** from gaming nights and fitness challenges to annual retreats at world\-class resorts, we make sure there’s never a shortage of fun and connection.

title: Oilseeds Product Manager
description:
**The Oilseeds Product Manager – EMEA/APAC** leads product marketing, technical product positioning, product life\-cycle management, performance interpretation, product concepts and portfolio optimization for sunflower and winter oilseed rape. The role also supports licensing product readiness, product selection, partner technical enablement and documentation. It connects Research output, field evidence, licensee and farmer needs, agronomic fit, competitive intelligence and commercial strategy into clear product decisions, launch plans, portfolio recommendations and practical guidance that improve sales effectiveness, partner confidence and profitable growth across EMEA/APAC.


**Key Responsibilities**

Product Strategy \& Product Marketing


* Support the Regional Portfolio Leader in building the regional oilseeds product strategy across EMEA/APAC, translating crop strategy, customer needs, agronomic requirements, market segmentation and competitive dynamics into clear product priorities.
* Define positioning by crop, maturity and technical segment, geography, growing environment, farming system, customer segment and end\-use, with a specific focus on the best portfolio offer for out\-licensing needs.
* Build strong value propositions, product stories, technical claims and competitive differentiation to support country marketing, category marketing, launch campaigns, sales tools, pricing discussions and technical documentation.

Product Life\-Cycle Management


* Lead product life\-cycle recommendations from concept and advancement to launch, growth, maturity, decline and phase\-out; define product roles such as launch, growth driver, stable leader, tactical, niche, replacement or phase\-out candidate.
* Recommend advancement, expansion, restriction, maintenance or discontinuation based on performance, market fit, profitability, supply feasibility and strategic relevance.
* Partner with Portfolio, Demand Management, Supply Chain and Out\-Licensing Marketing to manage replacement plans, portfolio simplification, production impacts, inventory, carry\-over, availability and market protection.

Product Performance \& Agronomy Expertise


* Develop a deep understanding of product performance across countries, environments, farming systems, management levels, customer segments and out\-licensing partner clusters.
* Use Research data, pre\-commercial small plots, DEMO trials, commercial results, farmer and partner feedback, claims, agronomic observations and competitive comparisons to identify strengths, risks, best\-fit environments and “do not position” situations.
* Act as regional agronomy reference for sunflower and winter oilseed rape, providing practical guidance on farming systems, crop management, adaptation, planting window, maturity fit, disease profile, stress tolerance, harvest timing and end\-use requirements.

Trials, Data Valorization \& Field Evidence


* Support the design, prioritization and interpretation of regional and country trial networks with Research and Agronomy so trials generate decision\-useful information for advancement, positioning, launch, licensing and life\-cycle decisions.
* Transform country trial data into product insights, technical presentations, decision aids, crop guides, commercial tools and sales\-enabling messages that improve farmer and partner confidence.
* Coordinate and participate in key pre\-commercial small plot tours, field days, product advancement reviews, technical crop tours and customer\-facing agronomy events; support digital trial and performance tools.

Product Positioning, Concepts \& Research Alignment


* Lead regional product concepts and positioning documents reflecting target geography, maturity, agronomic profile, stress tolerance, disease package, quality expectations, trait needs, end\-use, management system and commercial potential.
* Partner closely with Research to align breeding priorities with market needs, agronomic requirements and future portfolio gaps; provide structured feedback from trials, commercial performance, claims, field observations and competitive benchmarks.
* Ensure successful launches through clear storytelling, technical targeting, field evidence, differentiation and practical sales arguments adapted to local market realities.

Licensing Portfolio Strategy \& Governance


* Support the Regional Licensing Leader in licensing portfolio strategy, product selection, technical fit, partner readiness and value creation through portfolio gap filling, market access, tactical defense, second\-brand development, local partner leverage or life\-cycle extension.
* Identify products suitable for licensing, second\-brand use, local partner deployment or restricted\-market positioning; validate technical fit by geography, maturity segment, agronomic environment, customer segment, competitive position and partner capability.
* Ensure licensed products have clear positioning, documentation, agronomic guidance, restrictions, brand rules and performance evidence before deployment; review performance by partner, market, role, volume, value and strategic contribution.
* Recommend corrective actions, substitutions or phase\-out plans when licensed products underperform or no longer fit the portfolio strategy; feed lessons learned back into product strategy and portfolio management.

Training, Knowledge Transfer \& Cross\-Functional Leadership


* Design practical training on product positioning, crop agronomy, performance, competitive differentiation, licensing readiness and technical sales arguments for sales teams, promoters, country marketing, agronomy teams and partners.
* Create and disseminate product guides, crop insights, positioning sheets, launch toolkits, technical brochures, decision aids, customer\-facing presentations and partner\-facing documentation.
* Work closely with Research, Agronomy, Portfolio Leaders, Regional Marketing, Sales Effectiveness, Supply Chain, Demand Management, Quality, Field Seed Production, Country teams and Licensing; challenge assumptions constructively and drive fact\-based product decisions.

**What This Role Is**

* A product marketing, technical product, commercial agronomy and licensing portfolio leadership role connecting Research, Agronomy, Marketing, Licensing and Commercial execution.
* A role owning product positioning, product concepts, life\-cycle recommendations, performance interpretation, agronomic guidance and licensing product readiness.
* A role turning field evidence into simple market and partner guidance grounded in farmer needs, licensee needs, agronomic fit and business value.

**Qualifications \& Experience**

* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Agronomy, Crop Science, Plant Breeding, Agricultural Engineering, Agrobusiness, Marketing, Business or related field.
* 10\+ years of experience in agriculture, preferably seeds, oilseeds, product management, agronomy, technical marketing, licensing, portfolio management, Research interface or commercial crop leadership.
* Strong understanding of sunflower and/or winter oilseed rape production systems; licensing experience and knowledge of other crops such as corn, soybean or cotton are strong advantages.
* Ability to interpret product performance data, trial results, field observations, partner feedback and commercial feedback, and translate them into clear commercial, agronomic and partner\-facing recommendations.
* Knowledge of life\-cycle management, positioning, advancement, launch planning, licensing readiness, field deployment, seed production, supply constraints, registration/FTO and partner readiness; fluent written and spoken English; EMEA/APAC and digital trial tool experience preferred.

**Leadership Competencies**

Strong product, crop and licensing ownership; technical credibility with Research, Agronomy, Licensing, Sales, Country teams and partners; strategic portfolio mindset; ability to translate technical complexity into simple field and partner guidance; customer, farmer, licensee and sales\-team orientation; influence without authority; data\-driven decisions; constructive challenge; clear communication, training, product storytelling and partner engagement; bias toward clarity, action and business impact; ability to balance short\-term execution with long\-term portfolio health and manage channel complexity.


**Success Metrics**

* Clear and adopted oilseeds product positioning, value propositions, licensing readiness and agronomic guidance across priority countries, partners and market segments.
* Stronger product life\-cycle, advancement, licensing and replacement decisions aligned with Research, market needs, partner needs, demand planning and supply feasibility.
* Successful launches, increased licensing conversion, stronger trial data valorization, improved sales/promoter/partner confidence, stronger country and partner adoption of regional guidance and reduced portfolio complexity where appropriate.
* Clear technical documentation for product concepts, performance, positioning, agronomic recommendations and licensing readiness; stronger integration of product and agronomy input into portfolio, licensing and commercial execution.

description:

We are looking for a **Tender Specialist** to support **medium–low complexity public tenders and related contract activities**, with a strong focus on operational execution and administrative accuracy.


This is a **temporary,** **part‑time opportunity** to contribute to a specific project or business need. You will bring your expertise to a team that values clarity, accountability, and collaboration, providing operational support to ensure continuity, accuracy, and efficiency in tender and contract activities.

 **Location:** Italy (Gallarate)

**Contract type:** Temporary, Part‑Time

 **You’ll make a significant contribution in the following areas:**

* Support the operational management of medium–low complexity public tender procedures (e.g. RDOs, negotiated procedures, direct awards).
* Plan, prepare, and submit assigned tender procedures in line with internal processes and applicable regulations.
* Monitor assigned tender opportunities and proactively update stakeholders on status, requirements, and deadlines.
* Initiate new tenders, prepare tender boards, and manage the tender process from notification to submission using a structured, project\-management approach.
* Coordinate with relevant internal functions to ensure all tender qualifications and requirements are fulfilled.
* Prepare, consolidate, and manage tender documentation using approved templates and internal instructions.
* Perform formal, administrative, and completeness checks to ensure accuracy and compliance of tender submissions.
* Conduct final quality checks to ensure 100% completeness and correctness of documentation prior to submission.
* Manage the tender lifecycle within Model N.
* Apply tender legislation and internal guidelines in daily activities, escalating regulatory or procedural uncertainties when required.
* Evaluate tender processes and share lessons learned to improve future performance.
* Support post\-award contract activities, including preparation and submission of required contract documentation.
* Provide the sales organization with key tender contract information (e.g. start date, end date, awarded amount).
* Support communication with customer procurement departments, mainly related to documentation and administrative follow\-up.
* Ensure proper archiving and maintenance of all tender and contract documentation (pre\- and post\-award) in designated systems and repositories, in line with internal procedures, audit requirements, and regulatory standards.

 **What you will need:**

* Knowledge of tender legislation and end\-to\-end tender processes.
* Preferably a degree in business, legal studies, or another relevant field.
* Preferably **1–3 years of experience** in tender management or bid support; experience in life sciences, healthcare, or medical devices is a plus.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office.
* User\-level experience with SAP, Salesforce, and/or Model N (training provided where needed).
* Fluency in Italian and English.
* High accuracy and strong attention to detail.
* Ability to work independently and manage operational responsibilities.
* Ability to collaborate effectively with diverse stakeholders.
* Clear and structured communication skills.
* Solution\-oriented and proactive mindset.

 **What we offer:**

* A **part‑time schedule** compatible with studies, early‑career development, or personal commitments;
* A **temporary assignment** with clear objectives and scope;
* Exciting work environment where you will get space for your ideas, and you are encouraged to take challenges and grow;
* Hands‑on exposure to real tender and contract activities in a regulated environment;
* Support and guidance from experienced tender professionals;
* Responsibility for driving the growth of the business divisions in Italy;
* Careful and comprehensive induction;
* Realistic targets and competitive bonus scheme;
* Open, friendly and fair working atmosphere.
 **Our approach to diversity and inclusion**


We strive to have a diverse mix of people from different cultures, ages, geographies and genders, to reflect the world in which we operate and to facilitate innovative thinking across the business.

 **Interested**?


Please submit your CV and contact Angela Procopio if you have any questions about the role or wish to discuss opportunities at Mölnlycke further.

 **About Mölnlycke**  

Mölnlycke is a world\-leading medical products and solutions company that equips healthcare professionals to achieve the best patient, clinical and economic outcomes.Our business is organised in the four business areas Wound Care, Operating Room Solutions, Gloves and Antiseptics, where customer centricity, sustainability and digitalisation are at the heart of everything we do.  

Mölnlycke employs around 8,700 people. The company headquarters are in Gothenburg, Sweden and we operate in more than 100 countries worldwide. Mölnlycke is a founding partner of GoCo Health Innovation City, a world\-class innovation cluster for health.  

Since 2007, the company has been part of Investor AB, an engaged owner of high\-quality, global companies which was founded by the Wallenberg family in 1916\. For more information, please visit www.molnlycke.com and www.molnlycke.com/careers

title: Head of Innovation
description:
**Who We Are**

At Kyndryl, we run and reimagine the mission\-critical technology systems that drive advantage for the world’s leading businesses. We are at the heart of progress; with proven expertise and a continuous flow of AI\-powered insight, enabling smarter decisions, faster innovation, and a lasting competitive edge. For our people—Kyndryls—that means doing purposeful work that powers human progress. Join us and experience a flexible, supportive environment where your well\-being is prioritized and your potential can thrive.  



  

**The Role**

Intesa, a Kyndryl Group company, is looking for a **Head of Innovation** as part of its growth strategy and efforts to position itself as a hub for innovation and digital transformation.
  

**Role Mission**  

Lead the identification, design, and go\-to\-market strategy for new digital services, while accelerating the structural adoption of AI within the organization.
  

**Key Responsibilities**  

*Service Innovation*

* Continuous analysis of technological, regulatory, and market trends to identify opportunities for new services aligned with Intesa’s positioning
* Defining business cases, MVPs, and launch roadmaps for new solutions, in coordination with Product, Compliance, and Technology
* Managing the idea\-to\-market cycle: from validation with pilot customers to production rollout

*AI Evangelism \& Adoption*

* Promoting an AI culture within the company through training, demos, internal use cases, and communities of practice
* Scouting and evaluating AI tools, models, and frameworks applicable to internal processes and services offered
* Supporting business units in identifying high\-impact AI use cases and measuring results

**Requirements:**

* High school diploma or bachelor’s degree in Economics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field
* At least 3–5 years of experience in this role
* Excellent knowledge of English; a minimum B2–C1 level is required
* Goal\-oriented mindset and strong customer focus
* Excellent mediation skills, even in critical situations
* Problem\-solving skills and a structured approach to decision\-making

**Location: Turin\-Milan (with a remote work contract)**  

**What we offer:**

* A young and dynamic environment
* A highly flexible remote work contract
* Trust and respect for ideas and opinions: in our team, everyone is free to share and propose new ideas and solutions
* Community events and initiatives where you’ll have the opportunity to discuss company projects related to sustainability, inclusion, and education.

***Consent to data processing***  

*You can withdraw your consent at any time by sending an e\-mail to the following address: privacy\_mktg@intesa.it. Or, if you no longer wish to receive marketing e\-mails, you can unsubscribe by clicking on the relevant unsubscribe link in any e\-mail. Further information on procedures can be found in the INTESA Privacy Policy. By submitting this form, I declare that I have read and understood the INTESA Privacy Policy.*  

***Diversity\&Inclusion***  

*HR managers, HR recruiters and employees all have a responsibility to ensure that diversity and inclusion are respected during the selection process. Prejudices and unconscious stereotypes must not influence our selection processes. We look for the best candidates and the real value they can bring to the organisation. We value meritocracy. We value diversity, setting aside our prejudices. All persons should have the opportunity to be considered for employment regardless of their race, colour, religion, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, military service, pregnancy, childbirth and related medical conditions, or any other characteristic protected by law and our collective bargaining agreements. INTESA will endeavour to make reasonable accommodation of known physical or mental limitations of a qualified candidate with a disability, unless the accommodation would impose an undue hardship on the operation of our business.*  

  

***Acknowledgement of the Code of Ethics and Disciplinary Code***  

*Before submitting your application, you are invited to read the company's Code of Ethics, which can be found on our website, and to note that Intesa's disciplinary code complies with the relevant provisions of the CCNL metalmeccanici, the collective agreement for employees and managers of Intesa s.p.a.*

  

**Who You Are**

See the job description above  



  

**Being You**

The “Kyn” in Kyndryl means kinship, which represents the strong bonds we have with each other, our customers and our communities. We focus on ensuring all Kyndryls feel included and we welcome people of all cultures, backgrounds, and experiences. Even if you don’t meet every requirement, we encourage you to apply. We believe in growth, and we’re excited to see what you can bring. At Kyndryl, employee feedback has told us that our number one driver of employee engagement is belonging. That sense of belonging — being a valued, respected, trusted member of the team — is fundamental to our culture and fueling great experiences for our customers. This dedication to welcoming everyone into our company means that Kyndryl gives you the ability to thrive and contribute to our culture of empathy and shared success. That’s The Kyndryl Way.  



  

**What You Can Expect**

Your career with us isn’t just a job—it’s an adventure with purpose. We offer a dynamic, hybrid\-friendly culture that supports your well\-being and empowers you to grow. Our Be Well programs are thoughtfully designed to support your financial, mental, physical, and social health—because we know that when you feel your best, you do your best.
  

  

From your very first day, you’ll dive into impactful work that powers the systems our customers rely on every day. You won’t just contribute—you’ll make a difference, tackling meaningful projects that sharpen your skills and fuel your growth.
  

  

We’re here to champion your journey. With powerful tools to chart your career path, personalized development goals aligned with your ambitions, and continuous feedback to keep you inspired and on track, you’ll have everything you need to thrive and evolve. You’ll develop in\-demand skills to grow your career and achieve your ambitions with access to cutting\-edge learning opportunities—from certifications with Microsoft, Google, and Amazon to coaching and hands\-on experiences. And through it all, you’ll be part of a culture that values empathy, restless learning, and a devotion to shared success.
  

  

We want you to thrive here—and we’re committed to helping you do just that. Ready to make an impact? Join us and help shape what’s next.


**Get Referred!**

If you know someone that works at Kyndryl, when asked ‘How Did You Hear About Us’ during the application process, select ‘Employee Referral’ and enter your contact's Kyndryl email address.

title: Contract Negotiator
description:
Milan, Italy \| Full time \| Home\-based \| R1544808
  
**Job Overview**  

Develop the global contracting strategy and support the delivery of all required start\-up contracting activities for selected sponsors, studies or multi\-protocol programs as determined by the Sponsor’s requirements.  

  

**Sponsor Requirements:**  

**Budget building within France and UK.****Global contracts experience within Germany, Austria, Nordics, Poland, Italy and or Spain.**  

**Essential Functions*** Develop Coordinate the development of investigator grants and estimates, contracting strategies and proposal text to support the proposal development process.
* Develop contract language, payment language and budget templates as required as applicable to the position
* Utilize and maintain contracting systems, tools, processes, and training materials for selected sponsors, studies or multi\-protocol programs according to the Scope of Work and Project Plan, within the agreed project strategy.
* Ensure collaboration, including communication with sponsors, stakeholders and RSU regions and countries as applicable to the position, to successfully deliver the agreed project scope in compliance with sponsor requirements and/or the RSU Management Plan.
* Provide specialist legal, operational and financial contracting support to the Study Teams, as applicable to site agreements to facilitate efficient business development and initiation and maintenance of clinical trials, whilst enabling compliance with regulatory requirements.
* Assist with the creation and/or review of core scientific, technical and administrative documentation to support business development and enable study initiation and maintenance, as required.
* Contribute to the collection, interpretation, analysis and dissemination of accurate contracting intelligence to support assigned studies and the wider company, as required.
* Ensure overall contracting efficiency and adherence to project timelines and financial goals as applicable to site agreements
* Report contracting performance metrics and out of scope contracting activities as required.
* Work with Quality Management to ensure appropriate contract management and quality standards. Mentor and coach colleagues as required including the delivery of training materials as required.
* Deliver presentations to clients as required. As applicable, eEnsure accurate completion and maintenance of internal systems (with emphasis on CTMS), databases, tracking tools, timelines and project plans.

May take a proactive role in developing long standing relationships with preferred IQVIA clients/customers.  
* 

**Qualifications**  

**Bachelor's Degree Related field Req**  

* **5\-year relevant sponsor or clinical research organization clinical site contracting experience**, including demonstrable global and/or regional experience acting as a contract negotiator. Equivalent combination of education, training and experience.
* Good negotiating and communication skills with ability to challenge.
* Strong legal, financial and/or technical writing skills.
* Strong understanding of regulated clinical trial environment and knowledge of drug development process.
* Knowledge and ability to apply GCP/ICH and applicable regulatory guidelines.
* Knowledge of applicable regulatory requirements, SOPs and company’s Corporate Standards.
* Strong understanding of particular Sponsor or stakeholder need and ability to share this knowledge effectively with others.
* Ability to utilize metrics and then communicate these with study team to establish timelines for deliverables.

Any false statements, misrepresentations, or material omissions during the recruitment process will result in immediate disqualification of your application, or termination of employment if discovered later, in accordance with applicable law. We appreciate your honesty and professionalism.

title: Strategic Healthcare Field Applications Engineer
description:
**About Analog Devices**

Analog Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ: ADI ) is a global semiconductor leader that bridges the physical and digital worlds to enable breakthroughs at the Intelligent Edge. ADI combines analog, digital, and software technologies into solutions that help drive advancements in digitized factories, mobility, and digital healthcare, combat climate change, and reliably connect humans and the world. With revenue of more than $9 billion in FY24 and approximately 24,000 people globally, ADI ensures today's innovators stay Ahead of What's Possible™. Learn more at www.analog.com and on LinkedIn and Twitter (X) .

  

**Role Purpose**  

ADI is seeking a Strategic Healthcare Field Applications Engineer to serve as the primary technical interface for key medical and pharmaceutical customers. This role is responsible for driving strategic customer engagement from early architecture discussions through design\-in and production support, while helping position ADI for long\-term growth in healthcare applications.


The role bridges customers, account teams, Systems Engineering, and ADI Business Units. It requires both strong technical depth and the ability to translate customer platform needs into internal action, influencing solution direction and helping drive successful business outcomes.


**Key Responsibilities**

* Serve as the lead technical interface for strategic medical and pharmaceutical customers
* Drive early customer engagement to understand platform needs, technical requirements, and long\-term roadmap opportunities
* Lead customer discussions spanning chip\-level requirements, power applications, and system\-level architecture
* Identify new design\-win opportunities and help expand ADI content in strategic healthcare platforms
* Translate customer needs into clear priorities for ADI Business Units and Systems Engineering teams
* Support technical proposals, product evaluations, design reviews, and application development
* Drive resolution of technical issues throughout the customer lifecycle, from concept through mass production
* Partner closely with Account Managers to support strategic customer growth
* Build trusted relationships with customer technical experts, engineering leaders, and internal stakeholders
* Continue developing strong product, application, and system\-level knowledge in healthcare markets

**Minimum Qualifications**

* BS or MS in Electrical Engineering
* Experience in engineering, semiconductors, or customer\-facing technical roles
* Strong problem\-solving skills
* Strong communication, interpersonal, and organizational skills
* Ability to work independently and collaboratively across global teams
* Proven customer project management skills
* Willingness to travel up to 50%
* Valid driver’s license

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Experience supporting healthcare, medical device, or pharmaceutical customers
* Ability to engage customers at both component and full system level
* Strong system\-level thinking and ability to connect technical detail to business opportunity
* Experience working across cross\-functional teams including sales, systems, and product groups
* Self\-motivated, curious, and comfortable challenging the status quo
* Strong relationship\-building skills with both customer and internal teams

**Why This Role Matters**  

This role expands ADI’s strategic engagement in healthcare by combining deep technical support with customer influence, platform insight, and strong cross\-functional collaboration. It is a strong opportunity for someone who wants to grow their impact across medical and pharmaceutical customers while helping shape future business.


*For positions requiring access to technical data, Analog Devices, Inc. may have to obtain export licensing approval from the U.S. Department of Commerce \- Bureau of Industry and Security and/or the U.S. Department of State \- Directorate of Defense Trade Controls. As such, applicants for this position – except US Citizens, US Permanent Residents, and protected individuals as defined by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3\) – may have to go through an export licensing review process.*

*Analog Devices is an equal opportunity employer. We foster a culture where everyone has an opportunity to succeed regardless of their race, color, religion, age, ancestry, national origin, social or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, pregnancy, parental status, disability, medical condition, genetic information, military or veteran status, union membership, and political affiliation, or any other legally protected group.*

Job Req Type: Experienced  

Required Travel: Yes, 10% of the time  

Shift Type: 1st Shift/Days

title: Machinist - Sarta/o
description:
Vivienne Westwood is one of the most iconic British fashion brands, renowned for its heritage, innovative design, and commitment to culture and activism. We care deeply about our people and the environment, and our core values \- Gaia, Quality not Quantity, Activism, Culture \& Heritage \- guide us daily. If you share our passion for creativity, sustainability, and innovation, we’d love to hear from you.


**ABOUT THE ROLE**



Vivienne Westwood is currently looking for a machinist to support the Design team based in Milan Office. The machinist contributes to the Milan Studio team, being responsible for the making of garments from first calico to finished, high quality samples for seasonal collections, fashions Shows or occasionally VIP dressing/clients, with a strong focus on calicos for new developments.


**KEY REPSONSIBILITIES**


* Sewing calicos, toiles and high\-quality samples for the seasonal collections, Shows, possibly VIP dressing, MTOs and special projects.
* Work confidentiality across all product categories, including Jersey, Tailoring, Evening wear, Corsetry.
* Demonstrate versatility in advanced sewing techniques and a willingness to experiment with innovative methods and construction ideas.
* Create precise toiles for new developments, working closely with Pattern Cutters and Designers throughout each stage of development.
* Read, understand and accurately interpret patterns, ensuring correct construction and proportion.
* Execute exceptional hand and machine sewing, including beading and embroidery, ensuring consistently high craftsmanship.
* Recommend appropriate finishing techniques and construction solutions to support design intent and garment quality.
* Carry out garment alterations with meticulous attention to detail, ensuring fit, balance and finish are refined to the highest standard.
* Cut samples accurately, handling fabrics with care and understanding shrinkage, grain and fabric behaviour.
* Taking care of studio equipment (e.g. sewing machine, irons, buttonhole machine, etc.),ensuring safe and efficient operation.
* Monitor, check and replenish studio stock including fabrics, yarns, zips and other components as needed.
* Work effectively under pressure and adapt to extended hours during peak periods, such as pre\-show deadlines.
* Support fashion shows as required, including backstage work on last\-minute amendments and garment finalisation.
* Attend fittings when needed, providing technical insight and support during adjustments.
* Any other ad hoc tasks required.


**PROFILE \& KEY SKILLS**


* Expert sewing skills for high\-quality samples, toiles, and calicos across multiple product categories (jersey, tailoring, corsetry). Minimum 3 years’ experience in the role.
* Ability to read and interpret patterns accurately
* Strong garment construction and alteration skills
* Versatility in sewing techniques and openness to experiment with new ideas
* Effective collaboration with designers and pattern cutters;
* Italian language is **essential** and a good English language kowledge is required to relate with the Studio Team
* High attention to detail and commitment to quality finishing
* Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines, including extended hours during Fashion Shows
* Knowledge of sewing machine maintenance and studio equipment care
* Organisational skills for managing fabrics, trims, and studio stock


**WHAT OUR COMPANY OFFERS**



A stimulating, young, and supportive work environment that values individual wellbeing; daily tickets restaurant; health \& wellbeing support (including supplementary medical health insurance, accident insurance, employee psychological support platform); staff discounts; other additional benefits.


***Vivienne Westwood is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, colour, sex, religion, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, genetic information, pregnancy or any other protected characteristic as outlined by local government laws.***

description:

Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


Join Us as a Client and Portfolio Manager in our Clinical Supplies Team – Make an Impact at the Forefront of Innovation Thermo Fisher Scientific, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale.


Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life \- enabling our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while taking science a step beyond through research, development and delivery of life\-changing therapies. With clinical trials conducted in 100\+ countries and ongoing development of novel frameworks for clinical research through our PPD clinical research portfolio, our work spans laboratory, digital and decentralized clinical trial services. Your determination to deliver quality and accuracy will improve health outcomes that people and communities depend on – now and in the future.


We have a vacancy for **Clinical Supplies Client and Portfolio Manager/Associate Director** in the **EMEA** region. The role can be **office, hybrid or fully remote based**. (level hired will depend on experience)


The role Manages full GCS oversight for high complex global clinical trials. Ensures project/study activities are in compliance with company and client requirements. Mentors, trains and provides on\-going support to junior team members. May act as main contact and escalation point for assigned clients. Provides consultation services to clients and may develop and update client\-specific manuals. Builds and maintains client relationship.

**A day in the life**

* Develops client specific governance and/or standards for each assigned client.
* Meets with internal teams to coordinate efforts, provide recommendations and risks, and update project reports/spreadsheets.
* Integrates all GCS activities to support project strategy and compliance with GxP requirements.
* Participates in ongoing training on new regulations.
* Participates in aspects of the bidding process including providing insight, strategy development, proposal development and parameters for business deliverables.
* Represents the department internally and externally at meetings, strategic projects, new business/business development strategy calls, bid reviews, bid defence meetings and initiatives as per the business requirements.
* Participates in or leads process improvement initiatives. Actively supports the roll\-out of new processes within the department.
* Maintains and uses existing tools while continuously looking for improvement opportunities.
* Consults the client on the best strategy for management of the clinical trial.
* Builds strong internal and external network.
* May act as a back\-up for the functional manager

**Education and Experience:**

* Bachelor's degree or equivalent and relevant formal academic / vocational qualification.
* Previous experience that provides the knowledge, skills, and abilities to perform the job (comparable to 8\+ years).

*In some cases an equivalency, consisting of a combination of appropriate education, training and/or directly related experience, will be considered sufficient for an individual to meet the requirements of the role.*

**Knowledge, Skills and Abilities:**

* Excellent English and communication skills both written and verbal
* Exceptional interpersonal, planning, negotiation, influencing, organizational, problem solving, sense of urgency, critical thinking and decision\-making skills
* Demonstrated leadership and team building skills
* Strong working knowledge of Microsoft Office suite
* Sharp focus on customers and attention to detail
* Ability to work in team environment, as well as work completely independent
* Comprehensive understanding of Global Clinical Supplies (GCS) concepts and application to clinical trials including a strong understanding of logistics and material management processes
* Experience in Cell and Gene experience is an advantage
* Exceptional client building and 3rd party relationship management
* Excellent ability to provide customer service with the highest standards of quality and excellence
* Superb skill in bid preparation and bid defence
* Strong presentation skills and proven ability to represent the department


We offer competitive remuneration, annual incentive plan bonus, healthcare, and a range of employee benefits. Thermo Fisher Scientific offers employment with an innovative, forward\-thinking organization, and outstanding career and development prospects. We offer an exciting company culture that stands for integrity, intensity, involvement, and innovation! Our Mission is to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. Watch as our colleagues explain 5 reasons to work with us.


As one team of 100,000\+ colleagues, we share a common set of values \- Integrity, Intensity, Innovation and Involvement \- working together to accelerate research, solve complex scientific challenges, drive technological innovation and support patients in need. \#StartYourStory with PPD, part of Thermo Fisher Scientific, where diverse experiences, backgrounds and perspectives are valued.


Apply today! http://jobs.thermofisher.com
